objective: Enhance AgentOps with Anthropic-style long-running application harness behavior by adding clean-room replay proof reuse, artifact-visible mission control, evaluator-grade feedback loops, and stricter generated-state hygiene.
done_criteria: Use ag-7t6 as the execution-packet proof-path dependency after correcting the mistaken ag-6oh.3 tracker reference.
Mission control exposes a bounded artifact timeline for the active run.
RPI emits structured evaluator output that distinguishes pass, warn, and fail and can drive retries mechanically.
Repo-mode reverse-engineer and adjacent eval surfaces ignore generated/temp trees such as .tmp and .agents where appropriate.

test_levels.rationale: The work spans CLI API surface, evaluator state transitions, phased-run integration, and isolated replay/proof behavior.

ranked_packet.why: Created after live tracker verification showed ag-6oh.3 was unrelated; this is now the real execution-packet proof-path dependency.
Current repo-mode reverse-engineer selected .tmp/compound-engineer as the AgentOps CLI surface.
A transcript-outcome scoring primitive already exists and should be promoted rather than replaced.
Mission control already exposes events, runs, and state, making artifact-timeline extension the right scope.
Fork exhaustion on subprocess-heavy summarization is a warning for new evaluator/replay tooling.
